introduction: This spec defines the Personal Access Token (PAT) lifecycle handlers for apikit, covering the four CRUD endpoints (`POST /user/tokens`, `GET /user/tokens`, `GET /user/tokens/:token_id`, `DELETE /user/tokens/:token_id`) that allow users to create, list, retrieve, and revoke their own fine-grained scoped credentials. Note: requirements referencing `auth.PermissionRegistry`, `auth.GetAuthInfo()`, `auth.GetUserID()`, `auth.RequirePermission()`, `db.WithTx`, `db.WrapError`, `db.ErrNotFound`, `db.ErrConflict`, `db.FormatTime`, `db.ParseTime`, and `APIError()` are based on upstream spec interfaces and should be cross-checked against the installed packages before implementation.

glossary.APIError: The standard error response helper from Server Core (spec 01) that produces a consistent JSON error envelope with code and message fields.
glossary.APIGroup: The Echo route group returned by `server.APIGroup()` to which authenticated route handlers are registered.
glossary.Cache-Control: no-store: HTTP response header directive that prevents clients and intermediaries from caching the response.
glossary.CreatePATRequest: The Go struct representing the JSON request body for `POST /user/tokens`, containing name, permissions, and optional expires fields.
glossary.CreatePATResponse: The Go struct representing the HTTP 201 response for PAT creation, including the one-time plaintext token.
glossary.PAT: Personal Access Token — a user-created, fine-grained credential in the format `<prefix>_pat_<token_id>_<secret>`. Multiple per user, each with specific permissions.
glossary.PATHandler: The Go struct holding the database connection and PermissionRegistry dependencies for PAT lifecycle operations.
glossary.PATResponse: The Go struct representing the HTTP 200 response for list, get, and revoke operations — metadata only, never includes the plaintext secret.
glossary.PermissionRegistry: The thread-safe registry of valid `resource_type:action` pairs defined in the auth middleware spec. Used to validate permissions during PAT creation.
glossary.TokenPrefix: The build-time configurable variable `apikit.TokenPrefix` (default `"ak"`) used as the leading segment of the PAT token format.
glossary.auth.GetAuthInfo: Function from spec 05 auth middleware that retrieves the full authentication info (including credential type and permissions) from the Echo context.
glossary.auth.GetUserID: Function from spec 05 auth middleware that retrieves the authenticated user's ID from the Echo context.
glossary.auth.RequirePermission: Function from spec 05 auth middleware that enforces a specific `resource_type:action` permission on the authenticated credential.
glossary.db.ErrConflict: Sentinel error returned by the database layer (spec 02) when a uniqueness or constraint violation occurs.
glossary.db.ErrNotFound: Sentinel error returned by the database layer (spec 02) when a queried row does not exist.
glossary.db.FormatTime: The database helper from spec 02 that serializes a Go `time.Time` value to the string format expected by SQLite.
glossary.db.ParseTime: The database helper from spec 02 that parses a SQLite timestamp string into a Go `time.Time` value.
glossary.db.WithTx: The database helper from spec 02 that wraps operations in a SQLite transaction and automatically rolls back on failure.
glossary.db.WrapError: The database helper from spec 02 that translates raw SQLite errors into sentinel error types such as `db.ErrNotFound` and `db.ErrConflict`.
glossary.expires_days: The original expiry duration stored in the `pats` table (0, 30, 60, or 90 days). Used for internal/audit purposes only; not included in any API response.
glossary.generateSecret: Internal function that generates a cryptographically random 32-character string from `tokenAlphabet`.
glossary.generateTokenID: Internal function that generates a cryptographically random 8-character string from `tokenAlphabet`.
glossary.hashSecret: Internal function that computes the SHA-256 hash of a string and returns it as a lowercase hex-encoded string.
glossary.insertion order: The order in which the client submitted permissions in the create request. This order is preserved in DB storage and all API responses.
glossary.pats: The database table storing all PAT records including token_id, user_id, name, secret_hash, permissions, expires_days, expires_at, created_at, and revoked_at.
glossary.privilege escalation: An attempt by a PAT to create a new PAT with permissions beyond those the creating PAT itself holds. Prevented by validation during PAT creation.
glossary.resource_type:action: The permission format used by PATs. Each permission is a colon-separated pair of a resource type and an action (e.g. `users:read`, `keys:manage`).
glossary.revoked_at: The timestamp when a PAT was permanently revoked. NULL while the PAT is active. Once set via a conditional UPDATE, the PAT can no longer authenticate.
glossary.secret: The random 32-character alphanumeric string embedded in the PAT format. Characters drawn from `[a-z0-9]`. Stored only as a SHA-256 hash; never persisted in plaintext.
glossary.secret_hash: The SHA-256 hash (hex-encoded) of the PAT secret. Stored in the `pats` table. Used by the auth middleware for credential validation.
glossary.tokenAlphabet: The constant `"abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz0123456789"` — the 36-character set from which both `token_id` and `secret` characters are drawn.
glossary.token_id: The random 8-character alphanumeric identifier embedded in the PAT format. Characters drawn from `[a-z0-9]`. Used for database lookup and API path parameters. Stored in plaintext.
glossary.tokens:manage: The permission string required to create or revoke PATs. Part of the registered permissions in the `PermissionRegistry`.
glossary.tokens:read: The permission string required to list or get PAT metadata. Part of the registered permissions in the `PermissionRegistry`.
glossary.user_id: The identifier of the authenticated user, injected into the Echo context by the auth middleware and used as a filter column in all PAT queries.
glossary.\"cannot grant permission: <permission>\: Error message returned (HTTP 403) when a PAT attempts to create a new PAT with a permission that the creating PAT does not itself hold.
glossary.\"cannot grant permission: keys:manage\": Example error message returned (HTTP 403) when a PAT without `keys:manage` attempts to grant that permission to a new PAT.
glossary.\"expires must be 0, 30, 60, or 90\": Error message returned (HTTP 400) when the `expires` field in a CreatePAT request is not one of the allowed values.
glossary.\"expires_at\": null: JSON representation of a null `expires_at` field in a PAT response, indicating the PAT has no expiry (expires_days = 0).
glossary.\"internal server error\": Generic error message returned (HTTP 500) when an unrecoverable server-side error occurs, such as a database transaction failure.
glossary.\"invalid permission format: <permission: Error message returned (HTTP 400) when a permission string does not contain exactly one colon separator.
glossary.\"invalid request body\": Error message returned (HTTP 400) when the JSON request body cannot be decoded.
glossary.\"name is required\": Error message returned (HTTP 400) when the `name` field is missing or empty in a CreatePAT request.
glossary.\"name must be 255 characters or fewer\": Error message returned (HTTP 400) when the `name` field exceeds 255 characters in a CreatePAT request.
glossary.\"permissions are required\": Error message returned (HTTP 400) when the `permissions` array is missing or empty in a CreatePAT request.
glossary.\"token already revoked\": Error message returned (HTTP 400) when attempting to revoke a PAT whose `revoked_at` field is already set.
glossary.\"token not found\": Error message returned (HTTP 404) when the requested `token_id` does not exist or does not belong to the authenticated user.
glossary.\"unknown permission: <permission>\": Error message returned (HTTP 400) when a permission string is well-formed but not registered in the PermissionRegistry.
glossary.*echo.Group: Pointer to an Echo v4 route group. The `RegisterRoutes` method receives this type for route registration.
glossary.<TokenPrefix>_pat_<token_id>_<secret>: The full PAT token format string template showing how `apikit.TokenPrefix`, `token_id`, and `secret` are concatenated.
glossary.<permission>: Placeholder representing a specific `resource_type:action` permission string in error messages.
glossary.APIError(): The standard error response helper from Server Core (spec 01) that produces a consistent JSON error envelope with code and message fields.
glossary.DELETE /user/tokens/:token_id: HTTP endpoint for revoking a specific PAT by its token_id.
glossary.GET /user/tokens: HTTP endpoint for listing all of the authenticated user's PATs.
glossary.GET /user/tokens/:token_id: HTTP endpoint for retrieving a specific PAT's metadata by its token_id.
glossary.NewPATHandler(database *db.DB, registry: The constructor function signature for creating a PATHandler with its required dependencies.
glossary.POST /user/tokens: HTTP endpoint for creating a new PAT.
glossary.SET revoked_at = <now> WHERE token_id =: The conditional SQL UPDATE statement used for PAT revocation, ensuring only non-revoked tokens owned by the authenticated user are updated.
glossary.[]: An empty JSON array, returned by the ListPATs endpoint when the user has no PATs.
glossary.[a-z0-9]: Regular expression character class representing the 36-character token alphabet (lowercase letters and digits).
glossary.^[a-z0-9]+_pat_[a-z0-9]{8}_[a-z0-9]{32}$: Regular expression pattern matching a valid PAT token string.
glossary.apikit.TokenPrefix: The build-time configurable variable (default `"ak"`) used as the leading segment of all credential token formats.
glossary.auth.GetAuthInfo(): Function from spec 05 auth middleware that retrieves the full AuthInfo struct from the Echo context, including credential type and permissions.
glossary.auth.GetUserID(): Function from spec 05 auth middleware that retrieves the authenticated user's UUID from the Echo context.
glossary.auth.RequirePermission(c, \"tokens\", \": Invocation of the auth middleware permission check requiring `tokens:manage` permission on the authenticated credential.
Invocation of the auth middleware permission check requiring `tokens:read` permission on the authenticated credential.
glossary.created_at + expires_days * 24h: The formula for calculating `expires_at` from the creation timestamp and the expiry duration in days.
glossary.created_at DESC: SQL ORDER BY clause used to sort PATs in reverse chronological order (most recently created first).
glossary.crypto/rand: Go standard library package providing cryptographically secure random number generation, used for generating token_id and secret values.
glossary.crypto/sha256: Go standard library package providing SHA-256 hash computation, used for hashing PAT secrets before storage.
glossary.database: The `*db.DB` parameter in the `NewPATHandler` constructor, providing database access for PAT operations.
glossary.encoding/hex: Go standard library package for hexadecimal encoding, used to encode SHA-256 hash output as a lowercase hex string.
glossary.expires: The optional integer field in CreatePATRequest specifying the PAT expiry duration in days (0, 30, 60, or 90).
glossary.expires_at: The nullable timestamp field indicating when a PAT expires. NULL when `expires_days` is 0 (no expiry).
glossary.generateSecret(): Internal function call that generates a cryptographically random 32-character secret from `tokenAlphabet`.
glossary.generateTokenID(): Internal function call that generates a cryptographically random 8-character token_id from `tokenAlphabet`.
glossary.hashSecret(secret): Internal function call that computes the SHA-256 hash of the given secret string.
glossary.keys:manage: A built-in permission string registered in the PermissionRegistry, granting the ability to manage API keys.
glossary.name: The human-readable label field in a CreatePATRequest, used to identify the PAT. Non-unique, max 255 characters.
glossary.permissions: The array of `resource_type:action` strings in a CreatePATRequest or PAT response, defining the PAT's granted permissions.
glossary.registry: The `*auth.PermissionRegistry` parameter in the `NewPATHandler` constructor, used for validating permissions during PAT creation.
glossary.revoked_at IS NULL: SQL WHERE clause condition ensuring only non-revoked PATs are matched in conditional UPDATE operations.
glossary.token: The full plaintext PAT string (format: `<prefix>_pat_<token_id>_<secret>`) returned only in the CreatePATResponse.
glossary.token_id AND user_id: The dual-column filter used in PAT queries to ensure a user can only access their own PATs.
glossary.token_id AND user_id AND revoked_at IS N: The three-column filter used in the conditional UPDATE for PAT revocation.
glossary.users:read: A built-in permission string registered in the PermissionRegistry, granting read access to user profiles.

user_story.goal: construct a PATHandler with required dependencies and register its routes on the authenticated Echo group
user_story.benefit: PAT lifecycle endpoints are available and protected by the auth middleware

acceptance_criteria.action: is constructed via `NewPATHandler(database *db.DB, registry *auth.PermissionRegistry) *PATHandler`; if either parameter is nil the constructor panics with a descriptive message rather than returning a nil handler
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: non-nil `*PATHandler` when both parameters are non-nil

acceptance_criteria.action: registers `POST /user/tokens` → CreatePAT, `GET /user/tokens` → ListPATs, `GET /user/tokens/:token_id` → GetPAT, and `DELETE /user/tokens/:token_id` → RevokePAT on the provided `*echo.Group`
run the auth middleware before reaching handler logic, so unauthenticated requests are rejected by the middleware before any handler code executes
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: 401 for unauthenticated requests, handled by auth middleware

acceptance_criteria.action: set the `Cache-Control: no-store` response header on every response, including error responses
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP response always contains header `Cache-Control: no-store`

edge_cases.action: panics with a descriptive message identifying which parameter is nil, preventing silent misconfiguration at startup
edge_cases.return_contract: panic (not a returned error) — library code signals misconfiguration via panic at construction time, never silently accepts nil dependencies

user_story.goal: generate cryptographically secure token IDs and secrets, and hash secrets before storage
user_story.benefit: PATs are unguessable and plaintext secrets are never persisted

acceptance_criteria.action: generates an 8-character string drawn exclusively from `tokenAlphabet` (`[a-z0-9]`) using `crypto/rand`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: `(string, error)` — string of exactly 8 characters on success; non-nil error if `crypto/rand` fails

acceptance_criteria.action: generates a 32-character string drawn exclusively from `tokenAlphabet` (`[a-z0-9]`) using `crypto/rand`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: `(string, error)` — string of exactly 32 characters on success; non-nil error if `crypto/rand` fails

acceptance_criteria.action: computes the SHA-256 hash of the input string and returns it as a lowercase hex-encoded string using `crypto/sha256` and `encoding/hex`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: deterministic 64-character hex string; same input always produces the same output

acceptance_criteria.action: constructs the full PAT token string as `<TokenPrefix>_pat_<token_id>_<secret>` where `TokenPrefix` is `apikit.TokenPrefix`, `token_id` is 8 chars from `tokenAlphabet`, and `secret` is 32 chars from `tokenAlphabet`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: token string matching pattern `[a-z0-9]+_pat_[a-z0-9]{8}_[a-z0-9]{32}`

edge_cases.action: returns the error to the caller (CreatePAT handler); the handler aborts PAT creation and returns HTTP 500 with message `"internal server error"` via `APIError()`; no partial token material is stored or returned
edge_cases.return_contract: non-nil error propagated to caller; HTTP 500 at handler boundary
edge_cases.error_condition: `crypto/rand` returns an error during random byte generation

user_story.goal: receive clear validation errors when the PAT creation request body is malformed or contains invalid data
user_story.benefit: clients can correct their requests without ambiguity

acceptance_criteria.action: returns HTTP 400 with message `"name is required"` via `APIError()`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 400, JSON error envelope `{"error":{"code":400,"message":"name is required"}}`
acceptance_criteria.trigger: receives a request body with a missing or empty `name` field

acceptance_criteria.action: returns HTTP 400 with message `"name must be 255 characters or fewer"` via `APIError()`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 400, JSON error envelope with message `"name must be 255 characters or fewer"`

acceptance_criteria.action: returns HTTP 400 with message `"permissions are required"` via `APIError()`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 400, JSON error envelope with message `"permissions are required"`
acceptance_criteria.trigger: receives a request body with a missing or empty `permissions` array

acceptance_criteria.action: returns HTTP 400 with message `"invalid permission format: <permission>"` via `APIError()`, where `<permission>` is the offending string
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 400, JSON error envelope with message `"invalid permission format: <permission>"`
acceptance_criteria.trigger: receives a permission string that does not contain exactly one colon (malformed `resource_type:action`)

acceptance_criteria.action: returns HTTP 400 with message `"unknown permission: <permission>"` via `APIError()`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 400, JSON error envelope with message `"unknown permission: <permission>"`
acceptance_criteria.trigger: receives a permission string that is well-formed but not registered in the `PermissionRegistry`

acceptance_criteria.action: returns HTTP 400 with message `"expires must be 0, 30, 60, or 90"` via `APIError()`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 400, JSON error envelope with message `"expires must be 0, 30, 60, or 90"`
acceptance_criteria.trigger: receives an `expires` value that is not one of {0, 30, 60, 90}

acceptance_criteria.action: returns HTTP 400 with message `"invalid request body"` via `APIError()`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 400, JSON error envelope with message `"invalid request body"`
acceptance_criteria.trigger: receives a malformed JSON request body that cannot be decoded

acceptance_criteria.action: treats a missing `expires` field (omitempty) as a default of 90 days
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: `expires_at` in response equals `created_at` + 90 × 24 hours

edge_cases.action: sets `expires_at` to NULL in the `pats` table and returns `"expires_at": null` in the `CreatePATResponse`
edge_cases.return_contract: HTTP 201 with `"expires_at": null` in the response JSON

edge_cases.action: returns HTTP 400 with message `"expires must be 0, 30, 60, or 90"`
edge_cases.return_contract: HTTP 400, JSON error envelope with message `"expires must be 0, 30, 60, or 90"`
edge_cases.trigger: receives `expires` as a negative integer or a value such as 7, 365, or 999

edge_cases.action: validates all `permissions` entries and returns the first validation error encountered (format check before registry check); stops at the first invalid permission rather than accumulating all errors
edge_cases.return_contract: HTTP 400 with the first encountered validation error message

user_story.role: authenticated user whose credential is a PAT
user_story.goal: be prevented from creating a new PAT with permissions beyond those my own PAT holds
user_story.benefit: PATs cannot be used to silently expand their own scope

acceptance_criteria.action: checks that every requested permission is present in the creating PAT's own permissions list; if any requested permission is absent, returns HTTP 403 with message `"cannot grant permission: <permission>"` via `APIError()`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 403, JSON error envelope with message `"cannot grant permission: <permission>"` for the first offending permission
acceptance_criteria.state: the authenticated credential (from `auth.GetAuthInfo()`) is a PAT

acceptance_criteria.action: skips the privilege escalation check entirely; any permission registered in the `PermissionRegistry` may be granted without restriction
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: no HTTP 403 from privilege escalation; proceeds to creation if all other validations pass
acceptance_criteria.state: the authenticated credential is an API key (admin or regular, from `auth.GetAuthInfo()`)

acceptance_criteria.action: performs privilege escalation checks only after validating that all requested permissions are registered in the `PermissionRegistry` (registry validation precedes escalation check)

edge_cases.action: allows creating a new PAT requesting a subset such as [`users:read`] and rejects a request for [`users:read`, `keys:manage`] with HTTP 403 on `"cannot grant permission: keys:manage"`
edge_cases.return_contract: HTTP 201 for subset; HTTP 403 with message `"cannot grant permission: keys:manage"` for superset
edge_cases.state: the authenticated credential is a PAT with permissions [`tokens:manage`, `users:read`]

requirements.title: Create PAT — token generation, storage, and response

user_story.goal: create a new PAT and receive the full plaintext token in the response
user_story.benefit: I can use the token immediately, knowing I cannot retrieve the secret again later

acceptance_criteria.action: generates `token_id` via `generateTokenID()`, generates `secret` via `generateSecret()`, computes `secret_hash` via `hashSecret(secret)`, constructs the full token as `<TokenPrefix>_pat_<token_id>_<secret>`, calculates `expires_at` as `created_at + expires_days * 24h` (NULL when `expires_days` = 0), inserts the PAT record into the `pats` table using `db.WithTx`, and returns HTTP 201 with `CreatePATResponse`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 201, `CreatePATResponse` JSON with `token_id`, `name`, `token` (plaintext), `permissions` (insertion order), `expires_at` (nullable), `created_at`
acceptance_criteria.trigger: receives a valid `POST /user/tokens` request after all validations pass

acceptance_criteria.action: stores only `secret_hash` (SHA-256 hex) in the `pats` table; the plaintext `secret` is never written to the database, logs, or any persistent storage
preserves the `permissions` array in insertion order in both the `pats` table (as a JSON array) and in the `CreatePATResponse`; the order matches the order the client submitted
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: response `permissions` array order equals request `permissions` array order

acceptance_criteria.action: omits `revoked_at` and `expires_days` fields; a newly created PAT is never revoked, and `expires_days` is an internal field not exposed in any API response
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: response JSON has no `revoked_at` or `expires_days` key

acceptance_criteria.action: wraps the `pats` table INSERT in a `db.WithTx` transaction so that any failure causes automatic rollback with no partial state persisted
requires the `tokens:manage` permission by calling `auth.RequirePermission(c, "tokens", "manage")` before processing the request; API keys bypass this check as enforced by the auth middleware
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 403 if the PAT credential lacks `tokens:manage`

acceptance_criteria.action: allows duplicate PAT names for the same user without error; no uniqueness constraint is enforced at the handler or database level for the `name` field
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 201 for each creation even if `name` matches an existing PAT

edge_cases.action: returns HTTP 500 with message `"internal server error"` via `APIError()`; the transaction is rolled back automatically by `db.WithTx`; no token material (token_id, secret, hash) is returned or persisted; the generated token is discarded
edge_cases.return_contract: HTTP 500, JSON error envelope with message `"internal server error"`
edge_cases.error_condition: the `db.WithTx` transaction or `pats` table INSERT fails for any reason (e.g., SQLite error, constraint violation)

edge_cases.action: generates `token_id` and `secret` before opening the `db.WithTx` transaction so that a transaction failure discards the generated values cleanly without any intermediate state being partially visible

user_story.goal: list all my PATs including their metadata and status
user_story.benefit: I can see my full token history and audit which PATs are active, expired, or revoked

acceptance_criteria.action: queries the `pats` table for all rows matching the authenticated `user_id` (from `auth.GetUserID()`), ordered by `created_at DESC`, and returns the full list as a JSON array of `PATResponse` objects
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 200, JSON array of `PATResponse`; empty array `[]` if the user has no PATs

acceptance_criteria.action: includes all PATs regardless of status (active, expired, revoked) in the response; status is apparent from `expires_at` and `revoked_at` fields
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: response array includes expired and revoked PATs

acceptance_criteria.action: never includes `secret_hash`, plaintext secret, `token`, or `expires_days` in any element of the response array; only metadata fields are returned
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: response JSON has no `secret_hash`, `token`, or `expires_days` keys in any array element

acceptance_criteria.action: returns only PATs belonging to the authenticated user; PATs belonging to other users are excluded from the result by the `user_id` filter in the query
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: no PATs from other users appear in the response

acceptance_criteria.action: requires the `tokens:read` permission by calling `auth.RequirePermission(c, "tokens", "read")` before processing the request
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 403 if the PAT credential lacks `tokens:read`

edge_cases.action: returns HTTP 200 with an empty JSON array `[]` rather than HTTP 404 or null
edge_cases.state: the authenticated user has no PATs in the `pats` table

edge_cases.action: returns HTTP 500 with message `"internal server error"` via `APIError()`
edge_cases.return_contract: HTTP 500, JSON error envelope with message `"internal server error"`

user_story.goal: retrieve the metadata of a specific PAT by its token_id
user_story.benefit: I can inspect a particular token's permissions, status, and timestamps

acceptance_criteria.action: queries the `pats` table by both `token_id` (from path parameter) and `user_id` (from `auth.GetUserID()`), and returns the PAT metadata as a `PATResponse`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 200, `PATResponse` JSON with `token_id`, `name`, `permissions`, `created_at`, `expires_at`, `revoked_at`
acceptance_criteria.trigger: receives a valid `GET /user/tokens/:token_id` request where the `token_id` path parameter matches a PAT belonging to the authenticated user

acceptance_criteria.action: returns HTTP 404 with message `"token not found"` via `APIError()`; this applies whether the `token_id` does not exist at all or belongs to another user
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 404, JSON error envelope with message `"token not found"`
acceptance_criteria.trigger: the `token_id` does not exist in the `pats` table for the authenticated `user_id`

acceptance_criteria.action: requires the `tokens:read` permission by calling `auth.RequirePermission(c, "tokens", "read")` before processing the request
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 403 if the PAT credential lacks `tokens:read`

edge_cases.action: returns HTTP 404 with message `"token not found"` — not HTTP 403 — to avoid leaking the existence of the token to unauthorized callers
edge_cases.return_contract: HTTP 404, JSON error envelope with message `"token not found"`
edge_cases.trigger: a `token_id` that exists in the `pats` table belongs to a different `user_id` than the authenticated user

edge_cases.action: returns HTTP 500 with message `"internal server error"` via `APIError()`
edge_cases.return_contract: HTTP 500, JSON error envelope with message `"internal server error"`
edge_cases.error_condition: database query fails with an error other than `db.ErrNotFound`

user_story.goal: permanently revoke one of my PATs by its token_id
user_story.benefit: I can invalidate a compromised or no-longer-needed token while preserving the audit trail

acceptance_criteria.action: issues a conditional UPDATE: `SET revoked_at = <now> WHERE token_id = ? AND user_id = ? AND revoked_at IS NULL`; if the UPDATE affects one row, returns HTTP 200 with the updated `PATResponse` including the newly set `revoked_at`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 200, `PATResponse` JSON with `revoked_at` set to the revocation timestamp
acceptance_criteria.trigger: receives a valid `DELETE /user/tokens/:token_id` request for a PAT that exists, belongs to the authenticated user, and has not yet been revoked

acceptance_criteria.action: queries the `pats` table to distinguish two sub-cases: (a) if no row matches `token_id AND user_id`, returns HTTP 404 with message `"token not found"`; (b) if a row matches but has `revoked_at` already set, returns HTTP 400 with message `"token already revoked"`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 404 for case (a); HTTP 400 with message `"token already revoked"` for case (b)
acceptance_criteria.trigger: no row matches `token_id AND user_id AND revoked_at IS NULL`
acceptance_criteria.condition: the conditional UPDATE affects zero rows

acceptance_criteria.action: requires the `tokens:manage` permission by calling `auth.RequirePermission(c, "tokens", "manage")` before processing the request
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 403 if the PAT credential lacks `tokens:manage`

acceptance_criteria.action: does not delete the PAT row from the `pats` table; revocation only sets `revoked_at`, preserving the record for audit purposes and listing visibility
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: PAT row persists in `pats` table after revocation; `revoked_at` is non-null

edge_cases.action: the first UPDATE (which finds `revoked_at IS NULL`) succeeds and sets `revoked_at`; the second UPDATE finds zero matching rows and enters the disambiguation query, detecting that `revoked_at` is now set and returning HTTP 400 with message `"token already revoked"`; SQLite's single-connection pool makes this safe
edge_cases.return_contract: first request: HTTP 200 with `revoked_at` set; second request: HTTP 400 with message `"token already revoked"`
edge_cases.trigger: two concurrent `DELETE /user/tokens/:token_id` requests arrive for the same token

edge_cases.action: returns HTTP 404 with message `"token not found"` — not HTTP 403 — because the `user_id` filter is part of the WHERE clause and the token appears non-existent to the caller
edge_cases.return_contract: HTTP 404, JSON error envelope with message `"token not found"`
edge_cases.trigger: the `token_id` path parameter refers to a PAT belonging to a different `user_id`

edge_cases.action: returns HTTP 500 with message `"internal server error"` via `APIError()`
edge_cases.return_contract: HTTP 500, JSON error envelope with message `"internal server error"`
edge_cases.error_condition: the UPDATE or follow-up SELECT query fails with a database error

user_story.goal: ensure expired and revoked PATs remain visible in listings but cannot authenticate
user_story.benefit: users retain audit visibility while the system enforces access control correctly

acceptance_criteria.action: include expired PATs (where `expires_at` is in the past) in all list and get responses without filtering them out
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: expired PATs appear in responses with their original `expires_at` value

acceptance_criteria.action: include revoked PATs (where `revoked_at` is non-null) in all list and get responses without filtering them out
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: revoked PATs appear in responses with non-null `revoked_at`

acceptance_criteria.action: do not enforce authentication or expiry validation on PAT tokens; that responsibility belongs exclusively to the auth middleware (spec 05)

edge_cases.action: the PAT lifecycle handlers themselves do not check for blocked status; blocked-user enforcement is handled by the auth middleware before the handler is invoked, so blocked users' requests are rejected before any handler logic runs
edge_cases.return_contract: blocked user requests rejected by auth middleware before handler code executes

correctness_properties.title: Secret never persisted in plaintext
correctness_properties.invariant: the `pats` table row for that PAT contains `secret_hash` (SHA-256 hex of the secret) and never contains the plaintext secret; the plaintext secret appears only in the `CreatePATResponse` at creation time and in no other storage, log, or response

correctness_properties.title: User isolation: users cannot access each other's PATs
correctness_properties.for_any: PAT row in the `pats` table belonging to `user_id` A
correctness_properties.invariant: no `GET /user/tokens/:token_id` or `DELETE /user/tokens/:token_id` request authenticated as `user_id` B (B ≠ A) returns HTTP 200 for that token; both return HTTP 404

correctness_properties.title: Permissions insertion order preserved
correctness_properties.for_any: PAT creation request with a `permissions` array in some order
correctness_properties.invariant: the `permissions` field in all responses (`CreatePATResponse`, `PATResponse` from list, get, and revoke) for that PAT contains the same permissions in the same order as the creation request

correctness_properties.title: No privilege escalation via PAT-created PATs
correctness_properties.for_any: PAT credential with permissions set P attempting to create a new PAT requesting permissions set Q
correctness_properties.invariant: if Q is not a subset of P the request is rejected with HTTP 403; if the creating credential is an API key (not a PAT), no such restriction applies

correctness_properties.title: Revocation is idempotent-safe (conditional update wins)
correctness_properties.invariant: exactly one of any set of concurrent `DELETE /user/tokens/:token_id` requests for that token receives HTTP 200; all subsequent requests receive HTTP 400 with message `"token already revoked"`; the `revoked_at` timestamp is set exactly once

correctness_properties.title: Token format invariant
correctness_properties.invariant: the `token` field matches the regular expression `^[a-z0-9]+_pat_[a-z0-9]{8}_[a-z0-9]{32}$` where the leading segment equals `apikit.TokenPrefix`

correctness_properties.title: Cache-Control header on all responses
correctness_properties.invariant: the response includes the header `Cache-Control: no-store` regardless of HTTP status code (2xx or 4xx or 5xx)

execution_paths.title: Happy path: create a new PAT

steps.action: sends `POST /user/tokens` with JSON body containing `name`, `permissions`, and optional `expires`; request includes a valid API key or PAT credential in the Authorization header

validates the credential, injects authenticated `user_id` and credential info into the Echo context, and calls `auth.RequirePermission(c, "tokens", "manage")` — passes for API key or PAT with `tokens:manage`

decodes and validates the JSON request body: checks `name` non-empty and ≤255 chars, `permissions` non-empty, each permission is well-formed and registered in `PermissionRegistry`, and `expires` is in {0, 30, 60, 90}

checks privilege escalation: if credential is a PAT, verifies every requested permission is in the creating PAT's permissions list; returns HTTP 403 with `"cannot grant permission: <permission>"` on violation
calls `generateTokenID()` for an 8-char `[a-z0-9]` token ID and `generateSecret()` for a 32-char `[a-z0-9]` secret using `crypto/rand`

calls `hashSecret(secret)` to compute SHA-256 hex of the secret; constructs the full token string as `<TokenPrefix>_pat_<token_id>_<secret>`; calculates `expires_at` as `created_at + expires_days * 24h` (NULL if expires=0)

executes the INSERT into the `pats` table within a `db.WithTx` transaction, storing `token_id`, `user_id`, `name`, `secret_hash`, `permissions` (JSON array), `expires_days`, `expires_at`, `created_at`; rolls back automatically on any failure

returns HTTP 201 with `CreatePATResponse` containing `token_id`, `name`, the one-time plaintext `token`, `permissions` (insertion order), `expires_at`, and `created_at`; sets `Cache-Control: no-store` header
sends `GET /user/tokens` with a valid credential in the Authorization header
validates the credential and calls `auth.RequirePermission(c, "tokens", "read")`; injects `user_id` into context
retrieves `user_id` via `auth.GetUserID(c)` and queries the `pats` table for all rows matching `user_id`, ordered by `created_at DESC`
returns HTTP 200 with a JSON array of `PATResponse` objects (metadata only, no secrets, no `expires_days`); returns `[]` if no PATs exist; sets `Cache-Control: no-store` header

execution_paths.title: Happy path: get a single PAT

steps.action: sends `GET /user/tokens/:token_id` with a valid credential
validates the credential, calls `auth.RequirePermission(c, "tokens", "read")`, and injects `user_id` into context
extracts `token_id` from the URL path parameter and queries the `pats` table for the row matching both `token_id` AND `user_id`
if found, returns HTTP 200 with `PATResponse`; if not found (or belongs to another user), returns HTTP 404 with `"token not found"`; sets `Cache-Control: no-store` header
sends `DELETE /user/tokens/:token_id` with a valid credential
validates the credential, calls `auth.RequirePermission(c, "tokens", "manage")`, and injects `user_id` into context
extracts `token_id` from the URL path and issues a conditional UPDATE: `SET revoked_at = <now> WHERE token_id = ? AND user_id = ? AND revoked_at IS NULL`
executes the UPDATE and returns the number of rows affected

if 1 row affected: returns HTTP 200 with updated `PATResponse` including `revoked_at`; if 0 rows affected: queries `pats` to distinguish not-found (HTTP 404) from already-revoked (HTTP 400); sets `Cache-Control: no-store` header

execution_paths.title: End-to-end: PAT-authenticated create and revoke flow

steps.action: authenticates using an API key and calls `POST /user/tokens` to create a PAT with permissions [`tokens:manage`, `tokens:read`]
validates the API key credential and injects `user_id` and credential info into the Echo context

validates request, skips privilege escalation (API key credential), generates `token_id` and `secret`, computes `secret_hash`, inserts into `pats` via `db.WithTx`, returns HTTP 201 with `CreatePATResponse` including the plaintext `token`
stores the returned `token` value and uses it as the Authorization credential for subsequent requests
calls `DELETE /user/tokens/:token_id` using the PAT credential to revoke that same PAT

validates the PAT credential (parses token format, looks up `secret_hash` in `pats`, verifies hash matches, checks `tokens:manage` permission, verifies token is not expired or revoked), injects `user_id` into context
issues the conditional UPDATE setting `revoked_at`; returns HTTP 200 with `PATResponse` showing `revoked_at` set
observes that the revoked PAT now shows `revoked_at` in `GET /user/tokens` listing, confirming audit trail is preserved

error_handling.condition: Request body for `POST /user/tokens` has a missing or empty `name` field
error_handling.behavior: CreatePAT handler returns HTTP 400 with message `"name is required"` via `APIError()`
CreatePAT handler returns HTTP 400 with message `"name must be 255 characters or fewer"` via `APIError()`

error_handling.condition: Request body for `POST /user/tokens` has a missing or empty `permissions` array
error_handling.behavior: CreatePAT handler returns HTTP 400 with message `"permissions are required"` via `APIError()`

error_handling.condition: A permission string in the request does not contain exactly one colon (malformed `resource_type:action`)
error_handling.behavior: CreatePAT handler returns HTTP 400 with message `"invalid permission format: <permission>"` via `APIError()`

error_handling.condition: A permission string is well-formed but not registered in the `PermissionRegistry`
error_handling.behavior: CreatePAT handler returns HTTP 400 with message `"unknown permission: <permission>"` via `APIError()`
CreatePAT handler returns HTTP 400 with message `"expires must be 0, 30, 60, or 90"` via `APIError()`

error_handling.condition: JSON request body for `POST /user/tokens` cannot be decoded
error_handling.behavior: CreatePAT handler returns HTTP 400 with message `"invalid request body"` via `APIError()`

error_handling.condition: Authenticated credential is a PAT and requests a permission not present in its own permissions list
error_handling.behavior: CreatePAT handler returns HTTP 403 with message `"cannot grant permission: <permission>"` via `APIError()`

error_handling.condition: `db.WithTx` transaction or `pats` INSERT fails during PAT creation
error_handling.behavior: CreatePAT handler returns HTTP 500 with message `"internal server error"` via `APIError()`; transaction is rolled back automatically; no partial state persisted
CreatePAT handler returns HTTP 500 with message `"internal server error"` via `APIError()`; no token material is returned or stored
ListPATs handler returns HTTP 500 with message `"internal server error"` via `APIError()`

error_handling.condition: `token_id` path parameter in `GET /user/tokens/:token_id` does not match any PAT for the authenticated user (either nonexistent or belongs to another user)
error_handling.behavior: GetPAT handler returns HTTP 404 with message `"token not found"` via `APIError()`

error_handling.condition: Database query fails with an error other than `db.ErrNotFound` during `GET /user/tokens/:token_id`
error_handling.behavior: GetPAT handler returns HTTP 500 with message `"internal server error"` via `APIError()`

error_handling.condition: Conditional UPDATE for `DELETE /user/tokens/:token_id` affects zero rows and the follow-up SELECT finds no matching row for `token_id AND user_id`
error_handling.behavior: RevokePAT handler returns HTTP 404 with message `"token not found"` via `APIError()`

error_handling.condition: Conditional UPDATE for `DELETE /user/tokens/:token_id` affects zero rows and the follow-up SELECT finds a row with `revoked_at` already set
error_handling.behavior: RevokePAT handler returns HTTP 400 with message `"token already revoked"` via `APIError()`

error_handling.condition: Database UPDATE or follow-up SELECT fails with an error during `DELETE /user/tokens/:token_id`
error_handling.behavior: RevokePAT handler returns HTTP 500 with message `"internal server error"` via `APIError()`

error_handling.condition: `NewPATHandler` is called with a nil `database` or nil `registry` parameter
error_handling.behavior: Constructor panics with a descriptive message identifying the nil parameter; prevents silent misconfiguration at server startup

symbols.name: Group
symbols.notes: Echo route group; PAT routes are registered on *echo.Group via g.POST(), g.GET(), g.DELETE()

symbols.name: Context
symbols.notes: Echo request context passed to all handler functions; used to extract path params, decode body, and write responses

symbols.name: Sum256
symbols.notes: Used in hashSecret() to compute SHA-256 digest of the PAT secret

symbols.name: Read
symbols.notes: Used in generateTokenID() and generateSecret() to fill byte slices with cryptographically secure random bytes

symbols.name: EncodeToString
symbols.notes: Used in hashSecret() to convert the SHA-256 byte array to a lowercase hex string

symbols.name: Marshal
symbols.notes: Used to serialize the permissions []string array to JSON for storage in the pats table

symbols.name: Unmarshal
symbols.notes: Used to deserialize the permissions JSON array from the pats table back to []string
